prompt: Map the AI eval / observability / testing landscape in 2026: top players (Braintrust, LangSmith, Langfuse, Galileo, Patronus, Arize, Comet/Opik), their funding/valuation, what each owns, pricing, and the gaps no one fills well. Cite sources.
content:
**The AI eval/observability/testing landscape in mid-2026 features a mix of evaluation-first platforms, full-stack observability tools, open-source/self-hosted options, and specialized players.** It is consolidating around tracing/monitoring production agents, automated/LLM-as-judge evaluations, prompt/dataset experimentation, and integrations with frameworks like LangChain/LangGraph or OpenTelemetry.[[1]](https://www.braintrust.dev/articles/best-ai-observability-tools-2026)[[2]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/best-llm-observability-tools)

Key players (focusing on the listed ones) differentiate by strengths in evals vs. tracing/production monitoring, open-source licensing, framework ties, and deployment models (self-hosted vs. SaaS). Funding and valuations reflect strong investor interest in AI reliability infrastructure, with notable activity like acquisitions and large rounds in 2025–early 2026.

### Braintrust
- **Funding/Valuation**: Raised $80M Series B in Feb 2026 (led by ICONIQ, with a16z, Greylock, etc.), at an $800M valuation; total funding ~$124M. Earlier Series A brought it to $45M total.[[3]](https://www.braintrust.dev/blog/announcing-series-b)[[4]](https://www.instagram.com/p/DU6lJQuEZLt/?hl=en)[[5]](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/537301-00)
- **What it owns**: Evaluation-driven observability platform strong in prompt experimentation, datasets, CI/CD gates for evals, session grouping/tracing, and review interfaces. Best for teams prioritizing rigorous, repeatable evaluations alongside tracing; often positioned as best overall or eval-first.[[1]](https://www.braintrust.dev/articles/best-ai-observability-tools-2026)[[6]](https://latitude.so/blog/best-ai-observability-tools-agents-2026)
- **Pricing**: Tiered + usage-based (e.g., hobby tier with limits like 1M spans/month or 10K evals; paid scales with volume).[[7]](https://pydantic.dev/articles/best-ai-observability-platform)

### LangSmith (LangChain)
- **Funding/Valuation**: LangChain raised $125M Series B in Oct 2025 at $1.25B valuation (led by IVP, with Sequoia, Benchmark, etc.).[[8]](https://www.langchain.com/blog/series-b)
- **What it owns**: All-in-one platform tightly integrated with LangChain/LangGraph (tracing, prompt versioning, agent tracing, human review queues, Insights Agent). Strong for LangChain-native teams building/deploying agents.[[9]](https://mlflow.org/articles/top-llm-observability-tools-in-2026-a-pro-guide/)[[10]](https://galileo.ai/blog/best-llm-observability-tools-compared-for-2024)
- **Pricing**: Usage-based SaaS. Developer plan (free tier limited); Plus at $39/seat/month + pay-as-you-go; Enterprise custom (self-hosted/hybrid options).[[11]](https://www.langchain.com/pricing)

### Langfuse
- **Funding/Valuation**: $4M–$4.5M seed (2023, Lightspeed, La Famiglia, YC); acquired by ClickHouse in Jan 2026.[[12]](https://langfuse.com/press/press)[[13]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/ai-agent-observability-2026-tracing-monitoring-stack-guide)
- **What it owns**: Open-source (MIT) all-in-one observability/analytics for LLM apps; strong self-hosting, session threading, deep evals (LLM-as-judge + custom), manual annotation. Popular for teams starting out or needing open-source/self-hosted options.[[2]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/best-llm-observability-tools)[[14]](https://bigdataboutique.com/blog/llm-observability-tools-compared-langfuse-vs-langsmith-vs-opik)
- **Pricing**: Self-hosted free (hobby tier); cloud paid tiers based on usage.[[13]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/ai-agent-observability-2026-tracing-monitoring-stack-guide)

### Galileo
- **Funding/Valuation**: $45M Series B (Oct 2024, led by Scale VP; total ~$68M across rounds, including earlier Khosla involvement).[[15]](https://galileo.ai/blog/announcing-our-series-b)[[16]](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/galileo-raises-45m-series-b-funding-to-bring-evaluation-intelligence-to-generative-ai-teams-everywhere-302276383.html)
- **What it owns**: Evaluation intelligence/observability platform focused on accuracy, guardrails, production agent reliability, and unified workflows (evals + monitoring). Enterprise-oriented with Fortune 50 traction.[[10]](https://galileo.ai/blog/best-llm-observability-tools-compared-for-2024)
- **Pricing**: Tiered SaaS with usage-based elements (free tier; Pro ~$100/month billed yearly scaling with traces; Enterprise custom).[[17]](https://checkthat.ai/brands/galileo-ai)

### Patronus
- **Funding/Valuation**: ~$20M total ($3M seed + $17M Series A in 2024, led by Notable Capital/formerly GGV, with Lightspeed, Datadog).[[18]](https://www.patronus.ai/blog/announcing-our-17-million-series-a)[[19]](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/535544-29)
- **What it owns**: Automated evaluation and security platform for enterprise GenAI reliability, detecting/fixing LLM issues at scale, compliance-focused testing.[[18]](https://www.patronus.ai/blog/announcing-our-17-million-series-a)
- **Pricing**: Not prominently detailed in public sources (likely enterprise/custom SaaS).

### Arize (incl. Phoenix)
- **Funding/Valuation**: Significant raises including $70M Series C (2025); total ~$130M–$230M across rounds; Series D valuation reported around $742M (Apr 2026).[[20]](https://forgeglobal.com/arize-ai_ipo/)[[21]](https://arize.com/blog/arize-ai-raises-70m-series-c-to-build-the-gold-standard-for-ai-evaluation-observability/)
- **What it owns**: OpenTelemetry-native observability/evaluation (Phoenix open-source for local/self-hosted use); strong in RAG debugging, drift detection, clustering, production monitoring, and agent evals. Mature for ML/AI teams; cloud SaaS (Arize AX) available.[[9]](https://mlflow.org/articles/top-llm-observability-tools-in-2026-a-pro-guide/)[[13]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/ai-agent-observability-2026-tracing-monitoring-stack-guide)
- **Pricing**: Open-source Phoenix free; cloud/enterprise SaaS usage-based.

### Comet/Opik
- **Funding/Valuation**: Comet (parent) has raised ~$63M–$75M historically (Series B valuation ~$314M in 2021); Opik is its open-source LLM observability/eval offering.[[22]](https://forgeglobal.com/comet_ipo/)
- **What it owns**: Open-source (Apache 2.0) end-to-end platform for testing, evaluating (LLM-as-judge), and monitoring agentic systems; integrates with broader ML workflows; production alerting and optimization. Good for Comet users or low-code platforms.[[2]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/best-llm-observability-tools)[[23]](https://www.comet.com/site/products/opik/)
- **Pricing**: Generous free tier/open-source core; enterprise plans.

**Gaps no one fills well** (based on 2026 comparisons): 
- Seamless end-to-end loop from pre-production evals/red-teaming directly into production monitoring/alerting/CI/CD regression tests on a single platform (some excel at one layer but require stitching vendors).[[24]](https://www.confident-ai.com/knowledge-base/compare/top-ai-testing-platforms-evals-observability-red-teaming-2026)
- Deep, native support for complex/long-running multi-turn autonomous agents beyond basic tracing (e.g., replay, advanced debugging).
- Cost-effective scaling for high-volume production traces without heavy usage fees or vendor lock-in.
- Integrated adversarial red-teaming/security with automated eval pipelines for enterprises.
- Unified open-source + enterprise-grade features with strong OTel/MCP tracing and minimal framework lock-in for non-LangChain stacks.[[13]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/ai-agent-observability-2026-tracing-monitoring-stack-guide)[[25]](https://laminar.sh/article/2026-04-23-top-6-agent-observability-platforms)

The space is evolving quickly with acquisitions (e.g., Langfuse) and new funding, favoring platforms that balance evals, tracing, and deployment flexibility. Sources are primarily recent 2025–2026 articles, company announcements, and comparison roundups.
